data.text: I’m reading the pre-claim intake hook and the existing agent tests first so I can tie each acceptance criterion to concrete code, schema, and test coverage before I change anything. Then I’ll wire the shared schema into the prompt/decoder tests, add the CI target, and run the required Go and pre-commit checks.

# DDx CLI Makefile

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"

# Build directory
BUILD_DIR = build

# Platforms to build for
PLATFORMS = \
	darwin/amd64 \
	darwin/arm64 \
	linux/amd64 \
	linux/arm64 \
	windows/amd64

.PHONY: all build clean test test-full test-perf coverage-ratchet lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql

# Default target
all: clean deps test build

# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/.frontend-build-stamp

frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
	@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@touch internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
frontend-rebuild:
	@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
frontend-verify:
	@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
	@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
	missing=0; \
	for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
		path=".$$ref"; \
		if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
			echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
			missing=$$((missing+1)); \
		fi; \
	done; \
	if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
		echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  ok"

# Sync the canonical default-plugin skill tree (../library/skills/) and its
# manifest into internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/ so //go:embed (which
# cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks them up. Runs as a prereq
# of build/test/dev so contributors never see a stale embedded default
# package. rsync --delete keeps the skill destination in exact sync.
DEFAULT_PKG_SRC := ../library
DEFAULT_PKG_DST := internal/registry/defaultplugin/library
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/skills/ $(DEFAULT_PKG_DST)/skills/
	@cp $(DEFAULT_PKG_SRC)/package.yaml $(DEFAULT_PKG_DST)/package.yaml

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build frontend-verify
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "Building current platform..."
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
	@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
		OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
		ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
		echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
		GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
		if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
			mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
		fi; \
	done

# Install dependencies
deps:
	@echo "Installing dependencies..."
	go mod download
	go mod tidy

# Run tests
test: copy-skills
	@echo "Running tests..."
	go test -v ./...

# Run full test suite with race detector and coverage (mirrors CI; opt-in for local pre-push verification)
test-full: copy-skills
	@echo "Running full test suite (race + coverage)..."
	for var in $$(git rev-parse --local-env-vars 2>/dev/null); do unset "$$var"; done; \
	CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -race -cover ./...

# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
bench-graphql:
	@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
	@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
	go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
	@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"

# Enforce the aggregate coverage ratchet recorded in coverage-baseline.yml.
# Kept separate from `make test` because building a coverage profile across
# the full module is slower than the verbose smoke run developers use for
# fast feedback; CI / quality gates invoke this target explicitly.
COVERAGE_PROFILE ?= coverage.out
COVERAGE_BASELINE ?= coverage-baseline.yml
coverage-ratchet: copy-skills
	@echo "Running coverage ratchet (go test -coverprofile=$(COVERAGE_PROFILE))..."
	@go test -coverprofile=$(COVERAGE_PROFILE) ./...
	@go run ./tools/coverage-ratchet -profile $(COVERAGE_PROFILE) -baseline $(COVERAGE_BASELINE)

# Run perf tests (excluded from default `make test`; use this target or CI perf job)
test-perf:
	@echo "Running perf tests (excluded from default test run)..."
	go test -tags=perf ./internal/server/perf/... -v -timeout 5m

# Validate the shared bead record schema
bead-schema:
	@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
	go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1

# Validate the shared readiness-checks payload schema
readiness-schema:
	@echo "Validating readiness-checks schema..."
	go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestReadinessChecksSchema' -count=1

# Validate bundled skill metadata
skill-schema:
	@echo "Validating bundled skill metadata..."
	go run . skills check

# Run linter
lint:
	@echo "Running linter..."
	@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		golangci-lint run; \
	else \
		echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \
		go vet ./...; \
	fi
	@echo "Running routinglint (FEAT-006 anti-reintroduction gate)..."
	@go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...

# Install locally for development
install: build
	@echo "Installing DDx locally..."
	../install.sh --from-build $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx

# Clean build artifacts
clean:
	@echo "Cleaning..."
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

# Run the CLI (for development)
run:
	go run . $(ARGS)

# Format code
fmt:
	go fmt ./...

# Update dependencies
update-deps:
	go get -u all
	go mod tidy

# Create release archives
release: build-all
	@echo "Creating release archives..."
	@cd $(BUILD_DIR) && \
	for file in ddx-*; do \
		if [[ $$file == *.exe ]]; then \
			zip $${file%.*}.zip $$file; \
		else \
			tar -czf $$file.tar.gz $$file; \
		fi; \
	done

# Development server (watches for changes)
dev: copy-skills
	@echo "Starting development mode..."
	@if command -v air >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		air; \
	else \
		echo "Air not found. Install with: go install github.com/cosmtrek/air@latest"; \
		echo "Falling back to basic run..."; \
		go run . $(ARGS); \
	fi

# Obsidian integration targets
obsidian-migrate: build
	@echo "Migrating HELIX workflow to Obsidian format..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate workflows/helix

obsidian-validate: build
	@echo "Validating Obsidian format in HELIX workflow..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian validate workflows/helix

obsidian-test:
	@echo "Running Obsidian integration tests..."
	go test ./internal/obsidian/... -v
	go test ./internal/obsidian/converter/... -v
	go test ./internal/obsidian/validator/... -v

obsidian-dry-run: build

func TestPreClaimIntakePrompt_AsksForFitForPurposeValidatedReplacement(t *testing.T) {
	root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)

	prompt, err := buildPreClaimIntakePrompt(root, store, b)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	lower := strings.ToLower(prompt)

	assert.Contains(t, lower, "prompt-quality improvements")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "suggested_fixes")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "suggested_fixes must be a json array")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "flat string list")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "tractability")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "score")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "rationale")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "suggested_child_beads")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "waivers_applied")

	// Prompt must instruct the model to classify unclear cases as operator_required.
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "operator_required",
		"prompt must instruct classification as operator_required for unclear cases; got:\n%s", prompt)
	assert.NotContains(t, lower, "ambiguous_needs_human")
}

func TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema(t *testing.T) {
	root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)

	prompt, err := buildPreClaimIntakePrompt(root, store, b)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	lower := strings.ToLower(prompt)
	for _, want := range []string{
		"classification",
		"tractability",
		"score",
		"rationale",
		"readiness_checks",
		"suggested_fixes",
		"rewrite",
		"suggested_child_beads",
		"waivers_applied",
	} {
		assert.Contains(t, lower, want)
	}
	assert.NotContains(t, lower, "confidence")
	assert.NotContains(t, lower, "reasoning")
}

func TestPreClaimReadinessPromptSpecifiesOutputShapes(t *testing.T) {
	root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)

	prompt, err := buildPreClaimIntakePrompt(root, store, b)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	lower := strings.ToLower(prompt)
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "suggested_child_beads must be a json array of objects")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "prefer a json string array of numbered criteria")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "decoder tolerates a single string fallback")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "waivers_applied must be a json array")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "prefer waiver objects with reason, criteria, and evidence")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "decoder tolerates a flat string list fallback")
}

func TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_ForbidsScalarReadinessChecks(t *testing.T) {
	root := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestRoot(t)
	store, b := newPreClaimIntakeHookTestStore(t, root)

	prompt, err := buildPreClaimIntakePrompt(root, store, b)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, lower, "readiness_checks must be a json array")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "must not be an object or string")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "reason")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "verdict")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "evidence")
	assert.Contains(t, lower, "checkable_before_attempt")
}

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"

    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0

        fi

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks

      glob: "cli/**/*.go"

      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"

          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do

    go-build:

      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"

      skip:

    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"

The pre-claim prompt specifies the expected output shape in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:616-621`, while the Go consumer decodes `ReadinessChecks` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:68` and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:781-814`. Define `readiness_checks` in a shared JSON schema file in the agent package, make the prompt/schema/decoder tests consume the same schema, and add a CI-accessible Make target that fails on schema drift. DEPENDENCIES
Depends on the decoder child that canonicalizes bool/string/null/absent verdicts.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestReadinessChecksSchema` compiles the shared readiness-checks schema and validates fixtures for bool, string, null, and absent verdicts.
